visualReviews.summary: Alpha is the clearest and most scalable identity grammar; Beta is more expressive; both make the evidence chain and Indian relevance specific without borrowing authority.
Beta has the strongest asymmetric rhythm; Alpha is the most resolved and extensible. Gamma makes the checkpoint too dominant for a default family grammar.
Alpha preserves the most balanced accountability chain. Alpha and Beta keep synthetic status, uncertainty, owner, approval state, and next safe action visible without implying automated judgment.

Alpha and Beta reflow into a coherent source-to-review mobile sequence without visible overflow. The shared dark-checkpoint contrast veto cleared after the single focused revision; Gamma remained over-dominant and crowded.

direction-alpha.rationale: Aligned planes and labeled open seams remained recognizable.

direction-beta.rationale: Unequal measures and narrow Witness Joint intervals remained recognizable.

direction-gamma.rationale: Stacked evidence beside one oversized review stage remained recognizable.

direction-alpha.rationale: The joined plane topology survived replacement of the wordmark and copy.

direction-beta.rationale: Variable typographic measure and column staging survived identity removal.

direction-gamma.rationale: The theatrical checkpoint hierarchy survived identity removal.
